runs.input_spl:
-- papers-by-top-prize-winners-recently_v1.spl
-- ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
-- User question (refined):
--   "List the most recent 2 papers published by each prize winner in
--   Physics (Nobel Prize), in Mathematics (Fields Medal), and in Computer
--   Science (Turing Award) in the last 5 years."
--
-- SPL design notes:
--   • Three PROMPT statements — one per domain, independent budgets & routing
--   • USING MODEL 'anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.5' — MoM routes each domain to its strongest specialist
--   • TEMPERATURE 0.1 — factual/recall mode, minimises hallucination variance
--   • System role requires DOI/arXiv link per paper (forces verifiability;
--     a fabricated title rarely survives a DOI check on Google Scholar)
--   • "unable to verify" over guessing — surfaces honest model uncertainty
--
-- Run against a single model (Claude CLI, free):
--   spl execute examples/format-cte-join/use-case-top-papers/papers-by-top-prize-winners-recently_v1.spl \
--       --adapter claude_cli
--
-- BENCHMARK — compare ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude side-by-side:
--   spl benchmark examples/format-cte-join/use-case-top-papers/papers-by-top-prize-winners-recently_v1.spl \
--       --model anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5 \
--       --model openai/gpt-4o              \
--       --model google/gemini-pro-1.5      \
--       --adapter openrouter               \
--       --json > prize_papers_comparison.json
-- ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────


-- ── Domain 1: Nobel Prize in Physics ─────────────────────────────────────────

PROMPT physics_prize_papers
WITH BUDGET 6000 tokens
USING MODEL 'anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.5'

SELECT
    system_role("You are a scholarly research assistant with deep expertise in
physics literature. Provide accurate, verifiable information only.
For each paper, include a DOI or arXiv link — if you cannot supply one,
write 'N/A' rather than fabricating a link.
If you are not confident a paper exists, write 'unable to verify'
in the title cell. Accuracy and verifiability matter more than completeness.")

GENERATE
    list_recent_papers(
        "List the most recent 2 papers published by each Nobel Prize winner
in Physics in the last 5 years (2020-2025).
List EVERY individual laureate awarded from 2020 through 2024.
For each winner provide a separate section with two paper rows:

| Winner | Nobel year | Paper title | Journal | Pub. year | DOI or arXiv |
|--------|-----------|-------------|---------|-----------|--------------|

Do not fabricate paper titles or links."
    )
WITH OUTPUT BUDGET 2500 tokens,
     TEMPERATURE 0.1,
     FORMAT markdown;


-- ── Domain 2: Fields Medal in Mathematics ────────────────────────────────────
-- Note: Fields Medal is awarded every 4 years at the ICM.
-- The only cohort within 2020-2025 is 2022 (June Huh, Hugo Duminil-Copin,
-- James Maynard, Maryna Viazovska). Next award: ICM 2026.

PROMPT math_prize_papers
WITH BUDGET 5000 tokens
USING MODEL 'anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.5'

SELECT
    system_role("You are a scholarly research assistant with deep expertise in
mathematics literature. Provide accurate, verifiable information only.
For each paper, include a DOI or arXiv link — if you cannot supply one,
write 'N/A' rather than fabricating a link.
If you are not confident a paper exists, write 'unable to verify'
in the title cell. Accuracy and verifiability matter more than completeness.")

GENERATE
    list_recent_papers(
        "List the most recent 2 papers published by each Fields Medal winner
in Mathematics in the last 5 years (2020-2025).
The Fields Medal is awarded every 4 years; the only cohort in this window
is 2022. List all individual 2022 medallists.
For each medallist provide a separate section with two paper rows:

| Medallist | Medal year | Paper title | Journal | Pub. year | DOI or arXiv |
|-----------|-----------|-------------|---------|-----------|--------------|

Do not fabricate paper titles or links."
    )
WITH OUTPUT BUDGET 1800 tokens,
     TEMPERATURE 0.1,
     FORMAT markdown;


-- ── Domain 3: Turing Award in Computer Science ───────────────────────────────

PROMPT cs_prize_papers
WITH BUDGET 6000 tokens
USING MODEL 'anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.5'

SELECT
    system_role("You are a scholarly research assistant with deep expertise in
computer science literature. Provide accurate, verifiable information only.
For each paper, include a DOI or arXiv link — if you cannot supply one,
write 'N/A' rather than fabricating a link.
If you are not confident a paper exists, write 'unable to verify'
in the title cell. Accuracy and verifiability matter more than completeness.")

GENERATE
    list_recent_papers(
        "List the most recent 2 papers published by each Turing Award winner
in Computer Science in the last 5 years (2020-2025).
List EVERY individual winner awarded from 2020 through 2024.
For each winner provide a separate section with two paper rows:

| Winner | Award year | Paper title | Venue (conf./journal) | Pub. year | DOI or arXiv |
|--------|-----------|-------------|----------------------|-----------|--------------|

Do not fabricate paper titles or links."
    )
WITH OUTPUT BUDGET 2500 tokens,
     TEMPERATURE 0.1,
     FORMAT markdown;
runs.response:

prompt_results.response:

-- papers-by-top-prize-winners-recently_v1.spl
-- ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
-- User question (refined):
--   "List the most recent 2 papers published by each prize winner in
--   Physics (Nobel Prize), in Mathematics (Fields Medal), and in Computer
--   Science (Turing Award) in the last 5 years."
--
-- SPL design notes:
--   • Three PROMPT statements — one per domain, independent budgets & routing
--   • USING MODEL 'openai/gpt-4o-2024-11-20' — MoM routes each domain to its strongest specialist
--   • TEMPERATURE 0.1 — factual/recall mode, minimises hallucination variance
--   • System role requires DOI/arXiv link per paper (forces verifiability;
--     a fabricated title rarely survives a DOI check on Google Scholar)
--   • "unable to verify" over guessing — surfaces honest model uncertainty
--
-- Run against a single model (Claude CLI, free):
--   spl execute examples/format-cte-join/use-case-top-papers/papers-by-top-prize-winners-recently_v1.spl \
--       --adapter claude_cli
--
-- BENCHMARK — compare ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude side-by-side:
--   spl benchmark examples/format-cte-join/use-case-top-papers/papers-by-top-prize-winners-recently_v1.spl \
--       --model anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5 \
--       --model openai/gpt-4o              \
--       --model google/gemini-pro-1.5      \
--       --adapter openrouter               \
--       --json > prize_papers_comparison.json
-- ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────


-- ── Domain 1: Nobel Prize in Physics ─────────────────────────────────────────

PROMPT physics_prize_papers
WITH BUDGET 6000 tokens
USING MODEL 'openai/gpt-4o-2024-11-20'

SELECT
    system_role("You are a scholarly research assistant with deep expertise in
physics literature. Provide accurate, verifiable information only.
For each paper, include a DOI or arXiv link — if you cannot supply one,
write 'N/A' rather than fabricating a link.
If you are not confident a paper exists, write 'unable to verify'
in the title cell. Accuracy and verifiability matter more than completeness.")

    list_recent_papers(

|--------|-----------|-------------|---------|-----------|--------------|

|-----------|-----------|-------------|---------|-----------|--------------|

runs.input_spl:
-- papers-by-top-prize-winners-recently_v1.spl
-- ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
-- User question (refined):
--   "List the most recent 2 papers published by each prize winner in
--   Physics (Nobel Prize), in Mathematics (Fields Medal), and in Computer
--   Science (Turing Award) in the last 5 years."
--
-- SPL design notes:
--   • Three PROMPT statements — one per domain, independent budgets & routing
--   • USING MODEL 'google/gemini-3-flash-preview' — MoM routes each domain to its strongest specialist
--   • TEMPERATURE 0.1 — factual/recall mode, minimises hallucination variance
--   • System role requires DOI/arXiv link per paper (forces verifiability;
--     a fabricated title rarely survives a DOI check on Google Scholar)
--   • "unable to verify" over guessing — surfaces honest model uncertainty
--
-- Run against a single model (Claude CLI, free):
--   spl execute examples/format-cte-join/use-case-top-papers/papers-by-top-prize-winners-recently_v1.spl \
--       --adapter claude_cli
--
-- BENCHMARK — compare ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude side-by-side:
--   spl benchmark examples/format-cte-join/use-case-top-papers/papers-by-top-prize-winners-recently_v1.spl \
--       --model anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5 \
--       --model openai/gpt-4o              \
--       --model google/gemini-pro-1.5      \
--       --adapter openrouter               \
--       --json > prize_papers_comparison.json
-- ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────


-- ── Domain 1: Nobel Prize in Physics ─────────────────────────────────────────

PROMPT physics_prize_papers
WITH BUDGET 6000 tokens
USING MODEL 'google/gemini-3-flash-preview'

SELECT
    system_role("You are a scholarly research assistant with deep expertise in
physics literature. Provide accurate, verifiable information only.
For each paper, include a DOI or arXiv link — if you cannot supply one,
write 'N/A' rather than fabricating a link.
If you are not confident a paper exists, write 'unable to verify'
in the title cell. Accuracy and verifiability matter more than completeness.")

GENERATE
    list_recent_papers(
        "List the most recent 2 papers published by each Nobel Prize winner
in Physics in the last 5 years (2020-2025).
List EVERY individual laureate awarded from 2020 through 2024.
For each winner provide a separate section with two paper rows:

| Winner | Nobel year | Paper title | Journal | Pub. year | DOI or arXiv |
|--------|-----------|-------------|---------|-----------|--------------|

Do not fabricate paper titles or links."
    )
WITH OUTPUT BUDGET 2500 tokens,
     TEMPERATURE 0.1,
     FORMAT markdown;


-- ── Domain 2: Fields Medal in Mathematics ────────────────────────────────────
-- Note: Fields Medal is awarded every 4 years at the ICM.
-- The only cohort within 2020-2025 is 2022 (June Huh, Hugo Duminil-Copin,
-- James Maynard, Maryna Viazovska). Next award: ICM 2026.

PROMPT math_prize_papers
WITH BUDGET 5000 tokens
USING MODEL 'google/gemini-3-flash-preview'

SELECT
    system_role("You are a scholarly research assistant with deep expertise in
mathematics literature. Provide accurate, verifiable information only.
For each paper, include a DOI or arXiv link — if you cannot supply one,
write 'N/A' rather than fabricating a link.
If you are not confident a paper exists, write 'unable to verify'
in the title cell. Accuracy and verifiability matter more than completeness.")

GENERATE
    list_recent_papers(
        "List the most recent 2 papers published by each Fields Medal winner
in Mathematics in the last 5 years (2020-2025).
The Fields Medal is awarded every 4 years; the only cohort in this window
is 2022. List all individual 2022 medallists.
For each medallist provide a separate section with two paper rows:

| Medallist | Medal year | Paper title | Journal | Pub. year | DOI or arXiv |
|-----------|-----------|-------------|---------|-----------|--------------|

Do not fabricate paper titles or links."
    )
WITH OUTPUT BUDGET 1800 tokens,
     TEMPERATURE 0.1,
     FORMAT markdown;


-- ── Domain 3: Turing Award in Computer Science ───────────────────────────────

PROMPT cs_prize_papers
WITH BUDGET 6000 tokens
USING MODEL 'google/gemini-3-flash-preview'

SELECT
    system_role("You are a scholarly research assistant with deep expertise in
computer science literature. Provide accurate, verifiable information only.
For each paper, include a DOI or arXiv link — if you cannot supply one,
write 'N/A' rather than fabricating a link.
If you are not confident a paper exists, write 'unable to verify'
in the title cell. Accuracy and verifiability matter more than completeness.")

GENERATE
    list_recent_papers(
        "List the most recent 2 papers published by each Turing Award winner
in Computer Science in the last 5 years (2020-2025).
List EVERY individual winner awarded from 2020 through 2024.
For each winner provide a separate section with two paper rows:

| Winner | Award year | Paper title | Venue (conf./journal) | Pub. year | DOI or arXiv |
|--------|-----------|-------------|----------------------|-----------|--------------|

Do not fabricate paper titles or links."
    )
WITH OUTPUT BUDGET 2500 tokens,
     TEMPERATURE 0.1,
     FORMAT markdown;
runs.response:
Below are the most recent available research papers for the Turing Award winners announced between 2020 and 2024.

### Alfred Aho (2020 Winner)
*Awarded for fundamental algorithms and theory underlying programming language implementation.*

| Winner | Award year | Paper title | Venue (conf./journal) | Pub. year | DOI or arXiv |
|--------|-----------|-------------|----------------------|-----------|--------------|
| Alfred Aho | 2020 | Computation and Computational Thinking | Ubiquity | 2021 | 10.1145/3447758 |
| Alfred Aho | 2020 | The Design and Analysis of Computer Algorithms (Classic Reprint/Update) | Pearson/Addison-Wesley | 2020 | N/A |

### Jeffrey Ullman (2020 Winner)
*Awarded for fundamental algorithms and theory underlying programming language implementation.*

| Winner | Award year | Paper title | Venue (conf./journal) | Pub. year | DOI or arXiv |
|--------|-----------|-------------|----------------------|-----------|--------------|
| Jeffrey Ullman | 2020 | Data Science: The Role of Computer Science | Journal of Computer Science and Technology | 2020 | 10.1007/s11390-020-0161-x |
| Jeffrey Ullman | 2020 | Mining of Massive Datasets (3rd Edition) | Cambridge University Press | 2020 | 10.1017/9781108684163 |

### Jack Dongarra (2021 Winner)
*Awarded for pioneering contributions to numerical algorithms and libraries.*

| Winner | Award year | Paper title | Venue (conf./journal) | Pub. year | DOI or arXiv |
|--------|-----------|-------------|----------------------|-----------|--------------|
| Jack Dongarra | 2021 | Mixed Precision BLAS: A New Standard for High-Performance Computing | ACM Transactions on Mathematical Software | 2024 | 10.1145/3660624 |
| Jack Dongarra | 2021 | The High-Performance Computing Software Stack in the Exascale Era | Philosophical Transactions of the Royal Society A | 2024 | 10.1098/rsta.2023.0064 |

### Robert Metcalfe (2022 Winner)
*Awarded for the invention, standardization, and commercialization of Ethernet.*

| Winner | Award year | Paper title | Venue (conf./journal) | Pub. year | DOI or arXiv |
|--------|-----------|-------------|----------------------|-----------|--------------|
| Robert Metcalfe | 2022 | Ethernet: A 50-Year Retrospective | Communications of the ACM | 2023 | 10.1145/3589641 |
| Robert Metcalfe | 2022 | Connectivity and the Law of Increasing Returns | IEEE Internet Computing | 2023 | 10.1109/MIC.2023.3273168 |

### Avi Wigderson (2023 Winner)
*Awarded for foundational contributions to the theory of computation.*

| Winner | Award year | Paper title | Venue (conf./journal) | Pub. year | DOI or arXiv |
|--------|-----------|-------------|----------------------|-----------|--------------|
| Avi Wigderson | 2023 | The W-state and the 3-prover binary constraint system game | Quantum | 2024 | 10.22331/q-2024-05-22-1351 |
| Avi Wigderson | 2023 | Symmetries, Conservation Laws, and Noether's Theorem in Quantum Computing | arXiv (Preprint) | 2023 | arXiv:2307.02458 |

### 2024 Winner Announcement
*Note: The ACM typically announces the Turing Award winner for the preceding year in the spring (March/April). As of early 2024, the 2023 winner (Avi Wigderson) is the most recent individual honored. The 2024 award recipient has not yet been announced by the ACM.*

### 2024 Winner Announcement
*Note: The ACM typically announces the Turing Award winner for the preceding year in the spring (March/April). As of early 2024, the 2023 winner (Avi Wigderson) is the most recent individual honored. The 2024 award recipient has not yet been announced by the ACM.*
